title: Top BA (Hons. with Research) Journalism and Mass Communication College in Delhi NCR
content: The BA (Hons. with Research) in Journalism and Mass Communication is a 4-year undergraduate programme me aligned with NEP 2020, designed to shape ethical, skilled, and versatile media professionals. The course combines academic rigor with practical training in news writing, reporting, editing, multimedia production, and strategic communication across traditional and digital platforms. Students gain hands-on experience through internships, fieldwork, and live media projects, while also engaging with critical themes like media ethics, democracy, and representation. In the fourth year, eligible students (CGPA 7.5 or above) can undertake a faculty-guided research project or dissertation, earning a degree with Honours with Research. Graduates are equipped for roles in news media, digital platforms, communication firms, NGOs, and higher education or research. XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.A (Hons/Hons with Research) Journalism and Mass Communication Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate 01 To provide foundational and advanced knowledge in journalism, mass communication, and media studies relevant to today’s media landscape. 02 To develop professional skills in news writing, reporting, editing, visual storytelling, public relations, and digital media production. 03 To foster critical thinking, ethical awareness, and audience-centric communication across platforms such as print, broadcast, and digital media. 04 To prepare students for diverse media careers by offering hands-on training, industry exposure, and practical experience through internships and real-world projects. 05 To foster research and analytical skills through a final-year research project or dissertation, preparing students for leadership roles in media, communication, and further academic pursuits. 01 Media knowledge: Identify and interpret the media sources, message, channel and audience. 02 Content Development: Design/produce message/content for Print, Radio, Television, Film, and Multimedia as per the need of audience 03 Conduct investigations of complex problems: Integrate research-based knowledge and research methods including experiments, analysis and interpretation of media content and data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. 04 Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex media and communication problems (print, electronic and new media) with appropriate consideration for human rights, disaster management, data analytics, health communication, cultural, societal, and environmental communication. 05 Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques and tools to record, edit, share, broadcast/publish and analyse the media content (electronic, print and social media) with an understanding of the limitations. 06 Project management and finance: Synthesize knowledge and understanding of the media and communication principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage core media projects and in multidisciplinary environments. 07 Environment and sustainability: Elaborate the impact of the professional media and communication solutions for societal and environmental contexts; demonstrate the media knowledge for promotion of sustainable development. 08 Ethics: Integrate ethical principles and commitment to professional ethics, responsibilities and norms of the media practice. 09 10 11 01 Engage in resilient professional entrepreneurial activities to work as acknowledged leaders in media industry. 02 Work as media professionals with NGOs and Corporate for desired changes in society. 03 Undertake higher education in media industry and research organization. 01 Media Entrepreneurship: Develop entrepreneurial skills as visionary media professionals. 02 Multimedia Production: produce programs for multimedia platform. XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.A (Hons/Hons with Research) Journalism and Mass Communication Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate
